hero_subtitle: SFTY provides regulators a secure operational layer to integrate Travel Rule messages, transaction monitoring data, and KYT signals into audit-ready supervisory workflows.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Travel Rule data remains disconnected across systems
problem_description: Regulators struggle to assemble complete and consistent Travel Rule data from multiple sources, causing delays in supervisory review and gaps in transaction risk visibility. This fragmentation makes linking originator, beneficiary, and wallet activity difficult to trace and integrate into effective oversight workflows.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY integrates Travel Rule messages, wallet and transaction risk, provider outputs, and supervisory cases into a unified workflow that supports consistent review and evidence logging for regulators.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule messages to supervisory cases
workflow_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule message data, wallet and transaction risk signals into cases that support supervisory review, evidence logging and regulatory reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. A supervisory control plane
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented Travel Rule, KYT, wallet and transaction data into structured cases, evidence and reporting to support clear regulatory workflows and governance.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators with a secure, auditable platform featuring controlled access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe deployment for structured Travel Rule data exchange.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how Travel Rule data exchange supports supervision
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how Travel Rule workflows connect originator and beneficiary data
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how SFTY preserves audit-grade evidence for transaction monitoring
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe governance controls for secure regulatory data exchange environments
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY supports regulators by connecting Travel Rule data, wallet risk, transaction monitoring, cases and reports into a secure supervisory layer for structured oversight of digital assets.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule data?
faq_2_answer: SFTY consolidates Travel Rule messages, verifies completeness, manages exceptions and links originator and beneficiary data to transactions and supervisory cases for clear regulatory workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit logs, case histories, decision records and evidence-grade data to support supervisory reviews remain transparent and defensible within regulated environments.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple data providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY orchestrates multiple KYT, KYC, Travel Rule and blockchain analytics providers, normalizing their outputs into one integrated supervisory view without disrupting current systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing regulatory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY layers above existing regulatory and analytics tools, connecting their outputs into structured workflows rather than replacing underlying providers or established government systems.
final_title: Review secure supervisory data exchange workflows
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects Travel Rule, transaction monitoring and KYT data into governed, evidence-ready supervisory cases and reports.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
